1.1: Paulus, a Servant of Yeshua The Messiah, a called one, and an Apostle, who was separated to The Gospel of God,
1.2: Which from the first he had promised by his Prophets in the Holy Scriptures,
1.3: About his Son, who is begotten in the flesh from the seed of the house of David,
1.4: And was revealed The Son of God in power and by the Holy Spirit, who arose from the place of the dead, Yeshua The Messiah, Our Lord.
1.5: For in him we have received grace and Apostleship among all the nations, so that they would obey the faith of his name.
1.6: For you also are of them, called by Yeshua The Messiah.
1.7: To all who are in Rome, beloved of God, called and holy: Peace and grace be with you from God Our Father and from Our Lord, Yeshua The Messiah.
1.8: First, I thank my God in Yeshua The Messiah for all of you because your faith is heard in the whole world.
1.9: For God is calling me to testify, whom I serve by The Spirit in The Gospel of his Son, that without ceasing, I am always reminded of you in my prayers,
1.10: And I seek favor, that from now on, a way may be opened to me in the will of God that I may come to you,
1.11: Because I have greatly desired to see you and to give you a gift of The Spirit that you may be confirmed in him.
1.12: And we will be comforted together in your faith and mine.
1.13: But I want you to know, my brethren, that many times I have wanted to come to you and I was prohibited until now, that I also may have fruit in you like that in others of the Gentiles,
1.14: Greeks and Barbarians, wise and ignorant, for I owe a debt to preach to every person.
1.15: And so I take pains that also I may preach The Good News to you who are in Rome.
1.16: For I am not ashamed of The Gospel, because it is the power of God for the life of all who believe in it, whether of The Judeans first, or of the Aramaeans.
1.17: For the justice of God is revealed in it from faith to faith, according to that which is written: “The just one shall live by faith.”
1.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from Heaven against all the evils and the wickedness of the children of men, those who are holding the truth in evil.
1.19: Because a knowledge of God is revealed to them, for God has revealed it to them.
1.20: For the secrets of God from the foundation of the world are appearing to his creatures through intelligence, even his power and his eternal Godhead, that they will be without a defense,
1.21: Because they knew God, and they did not glorify him as God, nor did they give him thanks, so that they became destitute in their reasoning and became dull in their heart without understanding.
1.22: And when they thought in themselves that they were wise, they became insane.
1.23: And they changed the glory of God, who is indestructible, into the likeness of the image of man, which is destructible, and into the likeness of birds and of four footed animals and of creeping things of The Earth.
1.24: Because of this, God handed them over to the vile desires of their heart to disgrace their bodies among themselves.
1.25: And they exchanged the truth of God for lies, and they revered and served created things more than their Creator, to whom belong praises and blessings to the eternity of eternities, amen.
1.26: Therefore God handed them over to disgraceful diseases, and their females changed their natural need and became accustomed to that which is unnatural.
1.27: And again also their males in this way abandoned the natural need for females, and they were ravished with desire one for another, even male for male, and committed disgrace and received a right reward in their persons for their error.
1.28: And because they decided in themselves not to know God, God handed them over to a worthless mind so that they would be doing whatever is inappropriate,
1.29: As they are filled with every injustice and fornication, bitterness, wickedness, greed, envy, murder, contention, treachery and wicked reasoning,
1.30: Murmuring, slander, and they are detestable to God, being insolent, proud, boasters, inventors of evil, lacking reason, who do not obey their parents,
1.31: Because they have no stability, neither love, nor peace, nor compassion in them,
1.32: Who, while knowing the judgment of God, that those who commit such things are condemned to death, were not only doing these things, but also were attached to those who were doing these things.

2.1: Therefore, you have no defense, Oh man,who judges his neighbor, for that in which you judge your neighbor, of that you are guilty yourself, for you also who are judging are engaged in those things.
2.2: And we know that the judgment of God is in truth against those who are engaged in these things.
2.3: But why are you thinking, Oh son of man, who are judging those who are occupied in these things, that you will escape from the judgment of God, when you also are engaged in them?
2.4: Or do you presume upon the wealth of his sweetness and upon his patience and upon the place that he gave to you, and do not know that the sweetness of God brings conversion to you?
2.5: But because of the callousness of your heart which does not repent, you lay up for yourself the treasure of wrath for the day of wrath and the revelation of the just judgment of God,
2.6: He who pays every person according to his works:
2.7: To those who in the patience of good works are seeking glory, honor and indestructibility, he gives eternal life,
2.8: But those who resist and do not obey the truth, but obey evil, he will pay anger and fury,
2.9: Suffering and trouble, to every person who cultivates wickedness, to the Jews first and to the Aramaeans,
2.10: But glory, honor and peace to everyone who cultivates good, to the Jews first, and to the Aramaeans.
2.11: For there is no partiality with God.
2.12: For those who have sinned without The Written Law are also destroyed without The Written Law, and those who sinned with The Written Law will be judged by The Written Law.
2.13: The hearers of The Written Law are not righteous ones before God, but the doers of The Written Law are justified.
2.14: For if the Gentiles who have not The Written Law would perform those things of The Written Law by their nature, while they have not The Written Law, they would be The Written Law to themselves.
2.15: And they show the work of The Written Law written on their heart and their conscience testifies to them, while their reasoning rebukes or defends each one,
2.16: In the day when God judges the secrets of the children of men according to my Gospel, by Yeshua The Messiah.
2.17: But if you are called from the Jews and are comforted by The Written Law and are boasting in God,
2.18: You who know his will and discern what is right, you who are taught from The Written Law,
2.19: And you are confident yourself that you are a leader of the blind and a light of those who are in darkness,
2.20: And an instructor of those lacking understanding and a teacher of children, and you have a model of knowledge and of the truth in The Written Law,
2.21: You therefore who are teaching others, you do not teach yourself. You who are preaching that people should not steal, you are stealing!
2.22: You who say that people should not commit adultery, you commit adultery, and you who despise idols plunder the holy place!
2.23: And you who are boasting in The Written Law, you violate The Written Law, and you insult God himself!
2.24: For the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you, according to what has been written.
2.25: For circumcision is beneficial, if you will perfectly observe The Written Law, but if you depart from The Written Law, your circumcision becomes uncircumcision.
2.26: But if he of the uncircumcision will observe the commandments of The Written Law, behold, is not the uncircumcision accounted for circumcision?
2.27: And uncircumcision which by its nature perfectly observes The Written Law will judge you, who with the Scripture and with circumcision, violate The Written Law.
2.28: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, neither is that which is seen in the flesh circumcision.
2.29: But he is a Jew who is in secret, and circumcision is that which is of the heart, in The Spirit and not by the Scriptures, whose glory is not from the children of men, but from God.

3.1: What therefore is the excellence of the Jews, or what is the advantage of circumcision?
3.2: Much in everything; first, that they were entrusted with the words of God.
3.3: For if some of them did not believe, did they nullify the faith of God by not believing?
3.4: God forbid! For God is true, and every person lies, just as that which is written: “You will be upright in your words and you will be victorious when they judge you.”
3.5: But if our evil establishes the justice of God, what shall we say? Is God doing evil by bringing forth his wrath? I am speaking as a man.
3.6: God forbid! Otherwise, how will God judge the universe?
3.7: For if the truth of God is made to superabound for his glory by my lies, why therefore am I judged as a sinner?
3.8: Or is it as those, whose judgment is reserved for justice, slander us and report that we say, “Let us practice evil that good may come?
3.9: What, then? Are we held to be greater because we have precedence? We have determined about the Jews and about the Aramaeans that they are all under sin,
3.10: As it is written: “There is not a just person, not even one,”
3.11: “Neither is there one who understands, nor one who seeks God.”
3.12: “They have all turned away together, and they have been rejected, and there is not one who does good, not even one.”
3.13: “Their throats are opened tombs, their tongues are deceitful, and the venom of asps is under their lips.”
3.14: “Their mouth is full of curses and bitterness,”
3.15: “And their feet are swift to shed blood.”
3.16: “Adversity and wretchedness are in their way,”
3.17: “The way of peace they have not known,”
3.18: “And the awesomeness of God is not before their eyes.”
3.19: But we know that whatever things The Written Law has said, it has said to those who are into The Written Law, that every mouth may be shut and the whole universe may be guilty before God,
3.20: Because by works of The Written Law no one is justified before him, for by The Written Law sin has been made known.
3.21: But now the justice of God has been revealed without The Written Law and The Written Law and The Prophets testify of it.
3.22: But the righteousness of God is by the faith of Yeshua The Messiah unto every person, also upon every person who believes in him, for there is no distinction,
3.23: Because all of them have sinned and are deprived of the glory of God,
3.24: And are made right by grace without charge and by the redemption that exists in Yeshua The Messiah,
3.25: This One whom God preordained as the atonement, by the faith of his blood, for the sake of our sins which we had formerly sinned,
3.26: In the space that God in his patience has given to us, for the demonstration of his justice which is in this time, that he would be The Just One and would by justice declare righteous the one who is in the faith of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
3.27: Where is pride, therefore? It has been eliminated with him. By what law? Of works? No, but by the law of faith.
3.28: We determine therefore that by faith a man is made righteous and not by the works of The Written Law.
3.29: Is he The God of the Jews only? Is he not also of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also.
3.30: Because God is One, who declares the circumcision righteous by faith and also the uncircumcision by faith.
3.31: Are we eliminating The Written Law by faith? God forbid, but we are establishing The Written Law.

4.1: What therefore do we say about Abraham, the chief of our forefathers, that he found in the flesh?
4.2: For if Abraham were declared righteous by works, he would have had pride; but not in the presence of God.
4.3: For what do the Scriptures say? “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.”
4.4: But the wages of one who labors are not accounted to him as a favor, but as that which is owed to him.
4.5: But to the one who does not labor, but believes only in The One who justifies sinners, is his faith accounted for righteousness.
4.6: Just as David also said about the blessedness of a man to whom God accounts righteousness without works, as he said:
4.7: “Blessed are those whose evils are forgiven them and whose sins are covered.”
4.8: “And blessed is the man to whom God will not reckon his sins.”
4.9: Is this blessing therefore on the circumcision or on the uncircumcision? For we say “His faith was accounted to Abraham for righteousness.”
4.10: How therefore was it accounted to him, in circumcision or in uncircumcision? It was not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.
4.11: For he received circumcision as a sign and the seal of the righteousness of his faith, when in uncircumcision, that he would be the father to all those who believe among the uncircumcision, that it would be reckoned to them also for righteousness.
4.12: And he is the father to the circumcision, but not only to those who are from the circumcision, but also to those who follow the steps of faith of the uncircumcision of our father Abraham.
4.13: For it was not by The Written Law that The Promise came to Abraham and his seed, that he would be the heir to the universe, but by the righteousness of his faith.
4.14: For if these who are of The Written Law were the heirs, faith would have been worthless and The Promise would have been void.
4.15: For The Written Law is the worker of wrath, for where there is no Written Law, neither is there a violation of The Written Law.
4.16: Because of this, he would be justified by faith, which is by grace, and The Promise would be sure to all his seed, not to the one who is of The Written Law only, but also to the one who is of the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all,
4.17: According to what is written: “I have appointed you a father to the multitude of the nations, before God, in whom you believed, who gives life to the dead, and he calls those who are not as though they are.”
4.18: And without hope he believed in hope that he would be the father to the multitude of the nations according to what is written: “Thus shall your seed be.”
4.19: And he did not fail in his faith when he considered his body dead, (for he was 100 years old ), and the dead womb of Sarah.
4.20: And he did not doubt The Promise of God as if his faith were lacking, but he was strengthened in faith and he gave praise to God.
4.21: And he affirmed that whatever God promised him, He was able to perform.
4.22: Therefore, it was accounted to him for righteousness.
4.23: And this was not written for his sake alone that his faith was accounted for righteousness,
4.24: But also for our sake, for he is going to reckon it to us also, we who believe in The One who raised our Lord Yeshua The Messiah from the grave,
4.25: And who was handed over because of our sins; and he arose to justify us.

5.1: Because we have been declared righteous, therefore, by faith, we shall have peace with God in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah,
5.2: For in him we have been brought close by faith to this grace in which we stand, and we boast in the hope of the glory of God.
5.3: And not only in this way, but we boast also in afflictions, for we know that affliction perfects patience in us,
5.4: And patience, experience and experience, hope,
5.5: But hope does not disappoint, because the love of God has come in, overflowing our hearts by The Spirit of Holiness who has been given to us.
5.6: But surely because of our weakness, at this time The Messiah has died for the sake of the wicked.
5.7: For a man will hardly die for the sake of the wicked, however a man perhaps may dare to die for the sake of the good.
5.8: Here God demonstrates his love for us, because if when we were sinners, The Messiah died in our place,
5.9: How much more therefore, would we all the more be justified now by his blood and be saved from wrath by him?
5.10: For if when we were enemies, God was reconciled with us in the death of his Son, how much more therefore, in his reconciliation, shall we live all the more by his life?
5.11: And not in this way only, but also we glory in God by our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, by whom we have now received the reconciliation.
5.12: For just as by the agency of one-man, sin entered the universe, and by means of sin, death, in this way death passed by this sin unto all the children of men, because all of them have sinned.
5.13: For until The Written Law, sin was in the universe, but it was not accounted as sin, because there was no written law.
5.14: But death reigned from Adam and until Moses, even over those who had not sinned in the likeness of Adam's violation of the law, who was the image of him who was to come.
5.15: But the fall was unlike the gift in this way: for if because of the fall of one the many died, much more, therefore, the grace of God and the gift by The One Man Yeshua The Messiah shall superabound to the many.
5.16: And the gift is not the same as the offense of the one, for the judgment was from the one to pronouncing guilty verdicts, but the gift was from many sins to righteousness.
5.17: For if because of the offense of one, death reigned, all the more, those who receive an abundance of favor and the gift of righteousness shall reign in life by the agency of The One, Yeshua The Messiah.
5.18: In like manner therefore, because of the offense of the one there was a guilty verdict to all the children of men, in the same way, because of the righteousness of The One there shall be the victory for Life to all the children of men.
5.19: For just as because of the disobedience of one man the many became sinners, so also because of the obedience of The One the many become righteous.
5.20: But there was the introduction to The Written Law that sin would increase, and wherever sin increased, there grace superabounded.
5.21: That as sin reigned by death, in this way grace shall reign by righteousness to eternal life by our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.

6.1: What shall we say, therefore? Shall we remain in sin that grace may abound?
6.2: God forbid! For those of us who have died to sin, how shall we live in it again?
6.3: Or do you not know that those of us who were baptized into Yeshua The Messiah were baptized into his death?
6.4: We were buried with him in baptism into death, for as Yeshua The Messiah arose from among the dead in the glory of his Father, in this way also we shall walk in a new life.
6.5: For if we have been planted as one with him in the likeness of his death, in this way also we shall be in his resurrection.
6.6: For we know that our old person was crucified with him, that the body of sin would be destroyed, that we shall not again serve sin.
6.7: For whoever is dead has been freed from sin.
6.8: If therefore we are dead with The Messiah, let us believe that we shall live with The Messiah.
6.9: For we know that The Messiah arose from the place of the dead and he shall not die again, and death has no authority over him.
6.10: For he who died to sin, died one time, and because he lives, he is living to God.
6.11: In this way also consider yourselves that you are dead to sin and that you are living to God in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
6.12: Sin shall not therefore reign in your dead body so that you shall obey its desires,
6.13: Neither shall you present your members as weapons of evil for sin, but present yourselves to God as people who are alive from the dead and your members shall be weapons for the righteousness of God.
6.14: And sin has no authority over you, for you not under The Written Law, but under grace.
6.15: What, therefore? Shall we sin because we are not under The Written Law but under grace? God forbid!
6.16: Do you not know, that to whomever you give yourselves up to serve in bondage, his Servants you are, whom you obey, whether you listen to sin or to righteousness?
6.17: But thank God that you were Servants of sin and you obeyed from the heart that form of the teaching to which you are devoted.
6.18: And when you were freed from sin you became Servants to righteousness.
6.19: As a fellow man, I say to you because of the weakness of your flesh, that as you presented your members to the servitude of defilement and of evil, so also now present your members to the servitude of righteousness and of holiness.
6.20: For when you became Servants of sin, you became free from righteousness.
6.21: And what fruit did you have then, of which today you are ashamed? For its result is death.
6.22: And now because you have been freed from sin and you are Servants to God, your fruit is holy, for the result of those things is eternal life.
6.23: But the product of sin is death and the gift of God is eternal life in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.

7.1: Or do you not know, my brethren, for I speak to those who know The Written Law, that The Written Law has authority over a man as long as he lives,
7.2: As a woman is bound by the law to her lord as long as he lives? But if her husband is dead, she has been freed from The Written Law of her husband.
7.3: But if while her lord lives, she shall leave for another man, she becomes an adulteress to him, but if her lord should die, she has been freed from The Written Law, and she is not an adulteress if another man should have her.
7.4: And now, my brethren, you also have died to The Written Law with the body of The Messiah that you would be for another, The One who arose from the dead, that you would yield fruit to God.
7.5: When we were in the flesh, the diseases of sin, which are by The Written Law, worked diligently in the members that we might yield fruit to death.
7.6: But now we have been exempted from The Written Law, and we are dead to that which had controlled us, so that we shall serve from now on in the newness of The Spirit and not in the Old Order Scriptures.
7.7: What therefore shall we say? Is The Written Law sin? God forbid! But I would not have learned sin except by The Written Law, for I would not have known lust, if The Written Law had not said, “Do not lust.”
7.8: In this commandment sin found for itself an occasion and developed in me every lust, for without The Written Law, sin was dead.
7.9: But I was alive without The Written Law at first, but when the commandment came, sin lived, and I died.
7.10: And I found that commandment of life to be for death.
7.11: For sin, in the occasion that it found for itself, seduced me by the commandment, and killed me with it.
7.12: The Written Law therefore is holy and the commandment is holy, just and good.
7.13: Was the good therefore death to me? God forbid! But sin, that it might appear to be sin, perfected death in me by the means of the good, that sin would be all the more condemned by the commandment.
7.14: For we know that The Written Law is spiritual but I am carnal and I am sold to sin.
7.15: For that which I committed I did not understand, neither was it anything that I chose, but I was doing what I hated.
7.16: And if I did what I did not choose, I testify of The Written Law that it is excellent.
7.17: But now it is not I who am committing this, but sin that dwells within me.
7.18: For I know that good does not dwell within me, ( but this is in my flesh ), for it is easy for me to delight in the good, but I am unable to perform it.
7.19: It was not the good that I wanted that I did, but the evil that I did not want to do, that I did.
7.20: And if I did the thing that I did not want, it was not I doing it, but sin that dwelt within me.
7.21: I find, therefore, a law agreeing with my conscience which wants to do good, because evil is near me.
7.22: For I rejoice in the law of God in the inner person.
7.23: I saw another law in my members that makes war against the law of my conscience and takes me captive to the law of sin that is in my members.
7.24: I am a wretched man. Who will deliver me from this body of death?
7.25: I thank God by our Lord Yeshua The Messiah. Now therefore, I am a Servant of The Law of God in my conscience, but in my flesh, I am a Servant of the law of sin.

8.1: There is therefore no condemnation to those in Yeshua The Messiah who do not walk in the flesh.
8.2: For The Law of The Spirit of Life which is in Yeshua The Messiah has freed you from the law of sin and of death.
8.3: For because The Written Law was weak through the sickliness of the flesh, God sent his Son in the form of sinful flesh, because of sin, to condemn sin in his flesh,
8.4: That the righteousness of The Written Law would be fulfilled in us, that we would not walk in the flesh but in The Spirit.
8.5: Those who are in the flesh are governed by the flesh, and those who are of The Spirit are governed by The Spirit.
8.6: For the mind of the flesh is death, and the mind of The Spirit is life and peace,
8.7: Because the mind of the flesh is hatred toward God, for it is not subject to the law of God because it cannot be.
8.8: And those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
8.9: But you are not in the flesh, but in The Spirit, if truly The Spirit of God dwells within you, but if a man does not have The Spirit of The Messiah in him, this one does not belong to Him.
8.10: But if The Messiah is in you, the body is dead for the cause of sin, but The Spirit is alive for the cause of righteousness.
8.11: And if The Spirit of him who raised our Lord Yeshua The Messiah from among the dead dwells within you, he who raised Yeshua The Messiah from among the dead also will give life to your dying bodies, because of his Spirit who dwells within you.
8.12: Now my brethren, we are indebted, not to the flesh that we should walk in the flesh,
8.13: For if you are living in the flesh, you are going to die, and if you are putting to death the practices of the body, you are living.
8.14: For those who are led by The Spirit of God, these are the sons of God.
8.15: For you have not received The Spirit of bondage again to fear, but you have received The Spirit of the adoption of children by whom we cry, “Father, our Father.”
8.16: And that Spirit testifies to our spirit that we are sons of God;
8.17: And if sons, then also heirs; heirs of God and co-heirs with Yeshua The Messiah, for if we suffer with him, we shall also be glorified with him.
8.18: For I give counsel that the sufferings of this time are not comparable to the glory which is going to be revealed in us.
8.19: For the whole creation hopes for and expects the revelation of the sons of God.
8.20: For the creation has been subjected to futility, not by its choice, but because of him who subjected it upon hope.
8.21: For the creation shall also be freed from the bondage of destruction into the liberty of the glory of the sons of God.
8.22: For we know that all created things groan and are in labor until today.
8.23: And not only so, but we also who have in us the first fruits of The Spirit; we groan in ourselves and we look for the adoption of sons and the redemption of our bodies,
8.24: Because we live in that hope; but hope that is seen is not hope, for if we see hope, why do we look for it?
8.25: But if we hope for what is not seen, we wait patiently for it.
8.26: In this way also The Spirit helps our weakness. We do not know what we should pray for, whenever it is necessary, but that Spirit prays in our place with groaning which is unspoken.
8.27: But he who searches the hearts knows what the mind of The Spirit is, for he is praying according to the will of God in the place of the Saints.
8.28: But we know that he helps those who love God in everything for good, those whom he preordained to be called.
8.29: And those whom he foreknew, he also fashioned in the likeness of the image of his Son, that he would be The Firstborn of many brethren.
8.30: And those whom he pre-fashioned, he called, and those whom he called, he made righteous, and those whom he made righteous, he glorified.
8.31: What therefore shall we say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?
8.32: And if he did not show pity upon his Son, but he handed him over for the sake of us all, how shall he not give us everything with him?
8.33: Who shall accuse The Elect of God? God is He Who declares righteous.
8.34: Who is condemning? The Messiah has died and he is risen, and he is at the right hand of God and he prays for us.
8.35: What will separate me from the love of The Messiah: Suffering, or imprisonment, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
8.36: As it is written: “For your sake we are killed every day, and we are accounted as sheep for slaughter.”
8.37: But in all these things we are victorious by him who has loved us.
8.38: I am convinced that neither death, nor life, nor Angels, nor Authorities, nor Powers, nor things present, nor things future,
8.39: Nor height, nor depth, neither any other created thing, shall be able to sever me from the love of God, which is in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.

9.1: I speak the truth in The Messiah, and I do not lie; my conscience bears witness to me in The Spirit of Holiness,
9.2: That I have great unceasing sorrow and affliction from my heart,
9.3: For I have been praying that I myself might be destroyed from The Messiah, for the sake of my brethren and my kinsman, who are in the flesh,
9.4: Who are the children of Israel, whose was the adoption of children, the glory, The Covenant, The Written Law, the ministry which is in it, The Promises,
9.5: And the Patriarchs; and from them The Messiah appeared in the flesh, who is The God Who is over all, to Whom are praises and blessings to the eternity of eternities, amen.
9.6: But it was not that the word of God had failed, for they were not all of Israel, who are Israel;
9.7: Neither because they are of his seed are they all children of Abraham, because it was said, “In Isaac your seed shall be called.”
9.8: But this is not because the children of the flesh are the children of God, but the children of The Promise are accounted for the seed.
9.9: For The Promise is this word: “At this time, I will come and Sarah will have a son.”
9.10: And not only this, but Rebecca also, when she had a conjugal relation with our father Isaac,
9.11: Before her children were to be born or would do good or evil, the choice of God was revealed beforehand that this would stand not by works, but by him who called.
9.12: For it was said, “The elder shall be a Servant to the younger”,
9.13: As it is written: “I have loved Jacob and I have hated Esau.”
9.14: What shall we say, therefore? Is there evil with God? God forbid!
9.15: Behold, he said also to Moses, “I shall show love on whomever I love, and I shall take pity on whomever I pity.”
9.16: Therefore it is not by means of him who wills, neither by means of him who runs, but in the hand of God, the merciful.
9.17: For he said in the Scriptures to Pharaoh, “For this I have raised you up, that I may show my power with you, and that my name may be declared in the whole Earth.”
9.18: So then, he shows compassion on whomever he will, and whomever he will, he hardens.
9.19: Doubtless you will say, “Why does he find fault, for who stands against his will?”
9.20: Therefore, who are you, oh son of man, that you give a rebuttal to God? Does the thing formed say to the one who formed it, “Why have you made me this way?”
9.21: Or is not a potter authorized over the clay to make some formed things from it, one vessel for honor and one for dishonor?
9.22: But surely God was willing to show his wrath and reveal his power, bringing wrath with a multitude of patience against vessels of wrath that were perfected for destruction,
9.23: And his love overflowed on the vessels of compassion that were prepared by God for glory;
9.24: For we are called, not only from the Jews but also from the Gentiles.
9.25: Just as it says also in Hosea: “I shall call those who were not my people, 'My people', and you who were not beloved, 'Beloved'.
9.26: For it shall be in the place where they were called, “Not my people”, there they shall be called children of THE LIVING GOD.”
9.27: But Isaiah preached against the children of Israel: “If the number of the children of Israel is as the sand of the sea, a remnant of them shall be saved.”
9.28: He has cut the matter short and has cut it off; THE LORD JEHOVAH shall do it upon The Earth.
9.29: And as that which Isaiah said before: “If THE LORD JEHOVAH of Hosts had not left survivors, we would have been like Sadom and we would have been like Amorah.
9.30: What shall we say, therefore? The Gentiles, who did not run after righteousness have obtained righteousness, even the righteousness which is from faith,
9.31: But Israel that had run after The Written Law of righteousness did not obtain the Law of righteousness?
9.32: Why? Because it was not from faith, but from the works of The Written Law, for they were stumbled at the stumbling stone,
9.33: As it is written: “Behold. I have laid down in Zion a stumbling stone and a stone of offense and whoever will believe in him will not be disappointed.”

10.1: My brethren, the desire of my heart and my petition before God is on their behalf that they would have life.
10.2: For I bear witness to them, that they do have zeal for God, but not by knowledge.
10.3: For they have not known the righteousness of God, but they have sought to establish their own righteousness, and therefore they have not submitted to the righteousness of God.
10.4: For The Messiah is the consummation of The Written Law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
10.5: Moses wrote in this way of the righteousness which is in The Written Law: “Whoever shall do these things shall live in them.”
10.6: But the righteousness which is in faith says thus: “You shall not say in your heart, 'Who ascended to Heaven and sent down The Messiah?',
10.7: 'And who went down to The Abyss of Sheol and brought up The Messiah from among the dead?'”
10.8: But what does it say? “The answer is near your mouth and your heart.” This is the word of the faith that we preach.
10.9: And if you will confess with your mouth our Lord Yeshua, and you will believe in your heart that God has raised him from the dead, you shall have life.
10.10: For the heart that believes in him is made right, and the mouth that confesses him has life.
10.11: For the Scriptures say, “Everyone who believes in him will be unashamed.”
10.12: And he makes no distinction in this, not for the Jews, neither for the Aramaeans, for he is The One LORD JEHOVAH to all of them, who is rich with everyone who calls to him.
10.13: “For everyone who will call the name of THE LORD JEHOVAH shall be saved.”
10.14: Therefore, how would they call to This One unless they believed in him, or how would they believe him unless they heard him, or how would they hear without a preacher?
10.15: Or how will they preach, unless they will be sent, as it is written: “How beautiful are the feet of The Messengers of peace and of The Messengers of good things!”
10.16: But not all have submitted to the message of The Gospel, as Isaiah said, “My Lord, who believes our report?”
10.17: Therefore faith is from the hearing ear, and the hearing ear is from the word of God.
10.18: But I say, “Have they not heard?”, and, “Behold, their report has gone out into all The Earth and their words to the ends of the world.'”
10.19: But I say, “Did not Israel know beforehand?” Moses spoke thus: “I shall make you jealous by a people that is not a people and by a people that is disobedient I shall anger you.”
10.20: But Isaiah was bold and he said, “I appeared to those who did not seek me and I was found by those who did not ask for me.”
10.21: But to Israel he said, “I reached my hands out all day to a contentious and disobedient people.”

11.1: But I say, “Has God thrust away his people?” God forbid! I am also from Israel, from the seed of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
11.2: God has not thrust away his people, who were known to him from the first, or do you not know the Scriptures in which Elijah spoke when he complained to God about Israel and he said:
11.3: “My Lord, they have murdered your Prophets and they have toppled your altars, and I am left alone, and they seek my life”?
11.4: It was said to him by revelation, “Behold, I have left for myself 7000 men who have not bowed their knees and worshiped Baal.”
11.5: In the same way also at this time there is a remnant left in The Election of grace.
11.6: But if it is by grace, it is not from works, or else grace is not grace. But if it is by works, it is not from grace, or else work is not work.
11.7: Why therefore has not Israel found that which it was seeking, but The Election has found it and the rest of them were blinded in their hearts,
11.8: According to what is written: “God gave them a spirit of frustration, and eyes that will not observe and ears that will not hear”, even until today?
11.9: And again David said, “Their table shall be a trap before them and their reward, a stumbling block.
11.10: Let their eyes be darkened, lest they see, and may their back always be bent over.”
11.11: But I say, “Did they stumble so that they would fall? God forbid! But by their offense, life came to the Gentiles, to their envy.
11.12: And if their offense was wealth to the world, and their condemnation is wealth to the Gentiles, how much more therefore, their fullness?”
11.13: But I say to you Gentiles, I who am an Apostle of the Gentiles, I honor my ministry.
11.14: Perhaps I may make my kindred jealous, and I may save some of them.
11.15: For if their rejection was the reconciliation for the world, how much greater therefore is their return, if not life from the dead?
11.16: But if the first fruits are holy, so is the substance. And if the root is holy, so are the branches.
11.17: And if some of the branches were cut off and you, a wild olive tree, were grafted in their place, and you have become a partaker of the roots and of the fat of the olive tree,
11.18: Do not boast against the branches; but if you boast, were you not supported by the roots, rather than the root being supported by you?
11.19: Doubtless you will say, “The branches were cut off that I might be grafted in their place.”
11.20: Fine, these were cut off because they did not believe, but you have stood by faith. Do not be lifted up in your mind, but stand in awe.
11.21: For if God did not show pity upon the natural branches, surely he will not show pity upon you.
11.22: Behold therefore, the sweetness and the hardness of God; on those who fell, hardness, and on you, sweetness, if you abide in his sweetness, but if not, you will also be cut off.
11.23: And if they do not continue in their utter lack of faith, they will also be grafted in, for God is able to graft them in again.
11.24: For if you who are from a wild olive tree, which is your natural condition, were cut off and are grafted into the good olive tree apart from your natural condition, how much more will those surely be grafted into the olive tree of their own nature?
11.25: I want you to know my brethren, this mystery, lest you would be wise in your own opinion, that blindness of heart has for a little time come to Israel until the fullness of the Gentiles will come in.
11.26: And then all Israel shall have life, according to what is written: “The Savior shall come from Zion and he shall turn away evil from Jacob,
11.27: And then they shall have the covenant which comes from my presence, whenever I shall have forgiven their sins.”
11.28: But they are enemies in The Gospel for your sake, and in The Election they are beloved because of The Patriarchs.
11.29: For God does not change in his gifts and in his callings.
11.30: Just as you also were not obeying God from the first and now you have been favored because of their disobedience,
11.31: So also these have not obeyed, now mercy is upon you so that there shall be mercy upon them also.
11.32: For God has shut every person into disobedience so that he shall have mercy upon every person.
11.33: Oh, the depth of the wealth, the wisdom and the knowledge of God, for man has not searched his judgment and his ways are not traced!
11.34: For who has known the mind of THE LORD JEHOVAH, or who has been a counselor to him?
11.35: And who has first given to him and then has received from him?
11.36: Because all is from him, and in him, and all is by him, that to him would be praises and blessings to the eternity of eternities. Amen.

12.1: I beg you therefore, my brethren, by the mercies of God, that you present your bodies living sacrifices, holy and acceptable to God by a logical service.
12.2: And do not imitate this world, but be transformed by the renovation of your minds, and you shall distinguish what is the good, acceptable and perfect will of God.
12.3: I say to all of you by the grace that is given to me, that you should not have self-esteem beyond what is necessary to have self-esteem, but you should have self-esteem and modesty as God distributes faith to every person by a measure.
12.4: For just as we have many members in one body, and all those members do not have one function,
12.5: So also, we who are many, are one body in The Messiah, and each one of us is a member of the other.
12.6: But we have a variety of gifts, according to the grace that is given us; one has prophecy according to the measure of his faith,
12.7: And one has ministry in his service, and one has that of a teacher in his instruction.
12.8: One has that of a comforter, which is in his comforting, and a giver in generosity, and a top leader with diligence, and that of caregiver with cheerfulness.
12.9: And be not deceitful in your love, but hate evil and cleave to the good.
12.10: Be affectionate to your brethren and love one another; be preferring and honoring one another.
12.11: Be diligent and do not be lazy; be enthusiastic in spirit; be working for your Lord.
12.12: Be rejoicing in your hope. Bear your afflictions bravely. Be persistent in prayer.
12.13: Be partakers with the needs of the holy; befriend strangers.
12.14: Bless your persecutors; bless and do not curse.
12.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice and weep with those who weep.
12.16: And whatever you esteem about yourselves do also about your brethren. Do not esteem high opinions, but go out to those who are humble, and do not be wise in your opinions of yourselves.
12.17: Repay no person evil for evil, but be concerned with doing good before all people.
12.18: And if it is possible, according to what is within you, make peace with every person.
12.19: Do not avenge yourselves, beloved, but give place to rage, for it is written: “If you will not execute judgment for yourself, I shall execute your judgment, says God.”
12.20: “And if your enemy hungers, feed him, and if he thirsts, give him a drink, and if you do these things to him you will heap coals of fire on his skull.”
12.21: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil by good.

13.1: Let every soul be subject to the authority of the great, for there is no authority that is not from the same God, and those authorities who are from God are under orders.
13.2: Whoever therefore stands against the authority stands against the decrees of God, and these who stand against them shall receive judgment.
13.3: For judges are not a fear to the good doer, but to the wicked. Do you wish, therefore, to be unafraid of the authority? Do good, and you shall have praise from him.
13.4: For he is the Minister of God to you for good. But if you have done evil, be afraid, for he does not wear the sword for nothing, for he is the Minister of God and a furious avenger to those who do evil.
13.5: Therefore it is urgent for us to be subject, not for the sake of wrath only, but also for the sake of conscience.
13.6: For this cause, also, you pay the head tax, for they are the Ministers of God who are appointed for these things.
13.7: Pay every person what is owed to him: to whomever head tax, pay head tax, and to whomever a tribute tax, a tribute tax, and to whomever reverence, reverence, and to whomever honor, honor.
13.8: Owe no person anything but to love one another, for whoever loves his neighbor fulfills The Written Law.
13.9: For this also that says, “You shall not commit adultery”, “You shall not murder”, “You shall not steal, “You shall not covet”, or if there is any other commandment, it is summed up in the saying: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
13.10: Love does not commit evil against its neighbor, because love is the fulfillment of The Written Law.
13.11: I know this: Now is the time and the hour to awake from our sleep, for now our life has drawn closer to us than when we believed.
13.12: The night has passed and the day has arrived, therefore let us strip off the works of darkness from us, and let us put on the armor of light.
13.13: And let us walk in a right manner as those in the daytime, not in partying, not in drunkenness, not in orgies, not in envy or in fighting,
13.14: But put on our Lord Yeshua The Messiah and do not be concerned for the desires of your flesh.

14.1: But offer a hand to the one who is weak in faith and do not be divided by your disputes.
14.2: There is one who believes that he may eat everything, and he who is weak eats vegetables.
14.3: But let not him who eats despise him who does not eat, and let not him who does not eat judge him who eats, for God has accepted him.
14.4: Who are you to judge a Servant who is not yours? For if he stands, he stands to his Master, and if he falls, he falls to his Master, for it is appointed to his Master to be able to establish him.
14.5: There is one who distinguishes one day from another and there is another who judges all days the same, but let every person be certain in his own mind.
14.6: Whoever esteems whatever day, he esteems it for his Lord, and everyone who does not esteem whatever day, for his Lord he does not esteem it. He who eats, eats for his Lord and he gives thanks to God, and he who does not eat, for his Lord he does not eat, and he gives thanks to God.
14.7: For none of us lives for himself and no man dies for himself.
14.8: For if we live, we live for Our Lord, and if we die, we die for Our Lord; whether we live, therefore, or we die, we belong to Our Lord.
14.9: For this purpose also The Messiah died and lived and arose, that he would be THE LORD JEHOVAH to the dead and to the living.
14.10: But why are you judging your brother, or why do you even despise your brother? For all of us are going to stand before the judgment seat of The Messiah,
14.11: According to what is written: “As I live, says THE LORD JEHOVAH, every knee shall bow to me and to me every tongue shall swear.”
14.12: So then, every person among us gives an account of himself to God.
14.13: From now on let us not judge one another, but determine this rather: “You shall not lay a stumbling block for your brother.”
14.14: For I know and I am persuaded by THE LORD JEHOVAH Yeshua that there is nothing that is defiled in his presence. But to the one who regards anything impure, it is impure to him alone.
14.15: But if you grieve your brother because of food, you are not walking in love. You shall not destroy, by your food, one for whose sake The Messiah died.
14.16: And let not our good be blasphemed.
14.17: For The Kingdom of God is not eating and drinking, but the righteousness and the peace and the joy in The Spirit of Holiness.
14.18: For whoever serves The Messiah in these things is beautiful to God and is approved before the children of men.
14.19: And now, let us run after peace and after building one another up.
14.20: And let us not destroy a Servant of God because of food, for everything is pure, but it is evil to a man who eats with offense.
14.21: For it is good that we do not eat flesh, neither drink wine, neither anything by which our brother is subverted.
14.22: You who have faith in your soul, hold it before God. Whoever does not judge his soul in the thing which he designates is blessed.
14.23: For whoever is doubtful and eats is condemned because it is not in faith, for everything that is not from faith is sin.

15.1: Therefore, we who are strong are indebted to bear the infirmities of the weak and not to please ourselves.
15.2: But let each of us please his neighbor with good as for edification,
15.3: Because even The Messiah did not please himself, but according to what is written: “The reproach of those reviling you fell upon me.”
15.4: For everything that is written from ancient times is written for our teaching, that by patience and by comfort of the Scriptures we should have hope.
15.5: But The God of patience and comfort grant you that you may value one another with equality in Yeshua The Messiah,
15.6: That you may with one mind and one mouth glorify God The Father of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
15.7: Because of this, you shall accept and bear with one another, just as also The Messiah has accepted you for the glory of God.
15.8: But I say that Yeshua The Messiah ministered to the circumcision for the sake of the truth of God, so as to confirm The Promise of the fathers.
15.9: And the Gentiles shall glorify God for the sake of the mercies which have been upon them, as it is written: “I shall give thanks to you with the Gentiles and I shall sing praise to your name.”
15.10: And again it says, “Be delighted, you Gentiles, with his people.”
15.11: And again it says, “Praise THE LORD JEHOVAH, all you nations; praise him all peoples.”
15.12: And again, Isaiah said, “There shall be a root of Jesse and he who shall rise shall be The Ruler of the nations, and upon him the nations shall hope.”
15.13: But The God of hope shall fill you with all joy and peace by faith, that you shall superabound in his hope by the power of The Spirit of Holiness.
15.14: I am also convinced concerning you, my brethren, that you also are filled with goodness and you are filled with all knowledge and you are able also to instruct others.
15.15: But I have boldly written a few things to you, my brethren, so as to remind you by the grace that is given to me from God,
15.16: That I may be a minister to Yeshua The Messiah among the Gentiles and that I may labor for The Gospel of God, that the offering of the Gentiles may be accepted and sanctified by The Spirit of Holiness.
15.17: I have pride therefore in Yeshua The Messiah in the presence of God.
15.18: I dare not speak of anything to the audience of the Gentiles that The Messiah has not done through me by word and by works,
15.19: By the power of signs and wonders and by the power of The Spirit of God, just as I have gone around from Jerusalem unto Eluriqone and I have fulfilled The Gospel of The Messiah,
15.20: While I take pains to preach The Good News, not where the name of The Messiah is invoked, lest I build on an unusual foundation.
15.21: But just as it is written: “Those who had not been told about him shall see him, and those who have not heard shall be convinced.”
15.22: Because of this I was hindered many times when I would have come to you.
15.23: But now because I have no place in these regions, and I have desired for many years to come to you,
15.24: When I go to Espa§ia, I hope to come see you, and when a few of you would accompany me there, I shall be delighted to see you.
15.25: But now I go to Jerusalem to minister to the Saints.
15.26: But these who are in Macedonia and in Akaia were willing to share with the poor Saints who are in Jerusalem.
15.27: They were willing because they also are indebted to them, for if they have been made partners with them in the spiritual, the Gentiles are also indebted to serve them in the physical.
15.28: When therefore I have finished and have ratified this fruit to them, I am going to pass by you to Espania.
15.29: But I know that when I come to you, I shall come to you in the fullness of the blessing of The Gospel of The Messiah.
15.30: But I beg of you my brethren, by our Lord Yeshua The Messiah and by the love of The Spirit, that you labor with me in prayer to God for me,
15.31: That I would be delivered from those who are disobedient who are in Judea, and that the ministry I bring to the Saints in Jerusalem would be well received.
15.32: And I shall come to you in joy by the will of God, and I shall be refreshed with you.
15.33: Now may The God of Peace be with all of you. Amen.

16.1: I entrust Phoebe our sister to you, who is a Servant of the church of Qenkraus,
16.2: That you would accept her in Our Lord, as that is right for Saints, and in any matter that she seeks of you, that you would help her, because she is also a helper to many and also to me.
16.3: Invoke the peace of Priscilla and of Aqilaus, laborers who are with me in Yeshua The Messiah;
16.4: And they have yielded their necks for my sake, and I do not thank them only, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
16.5: And give greetings to the church that is in their house. Invoke the peace of Epentos my beloved, who is the first fruits of Akaia in The Messiah.
16.6: Invoke the peace of Maria, who has labored much among you.
16.7: Invoke the peace of Andronicus and of Junia, my relatives who were captives with me and were known by The Apostles and they were in The Messiah before me.
16.8: Invoke the peace of Amphilius, beloved in Our Lord.
16.9: Invoke the peace of Eurbanus, who is with us in The Messiah, and Estakus my beloved.
16.10: Invoke the peace of Appela, chosen in Our Lord. Invoke the peace of the members in the house of Aristobulus.
16.11: Invoke the peace of Herodion, my brother.
16.12: Invoke the peace of Trupana and Trupsa who labored in Our Lord; invoke the peace of Persis my beloved, who labored much in Our Lord.
16.13: Invoke the peace of Rufus, chosen in Our Lord, and of his mother and mine.
16.14: Invoke the peace of Asunqritus and of Plagon and of the brethren who are with them, and of Harma and of Petroba and of Harme.
16.15: Invoke the peace of Pillagus and of Julia, of Nereus, of his sister and of Alumpa and of all The Holy Ones with them.
16.16: Invoke the peace of one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of The Messiah invoke your peace.
16.17: But I beg of you my brethren, beware of those who are causing division and subversion outside of the doctrine which you have learned, that you should stay away from them.
16.18: For those who are such are not serving our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, but their own belly, and with sweet words and with blessings they deceive the hearts of the pure.
16.19: But your obedience has been made known to everyone. I rejoice therefore in you and desire that you would be wise to what is good and innocent to what is evil.
16.20: But The God of peace shall soon crush Satan under your feet. May the grace of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah be with you.
16.21: Timotheus, a worker with me invokes your peace, and Luqios, and Aison and Sosipatros, my brothers.
16.22: I, Tertius, invoke your peace, who have written the epistle by Our Lord.
16.23: Gaius, who receives me and the whole church, invokes your peace. Aristus, steward of the city, and Quertus a brother, invokes your peace.
16.24: But unto God who is able to confirm you by my Gospel, which has been preached concerning Yeshua The Messiah, by the revelation of the mystery, which from the times of the world has been hidden,
16.25: But has been revealed in this time by the Scriptures of The Prophets, and has been taught to all the nations by the commandment of The Eternal God for the hearing sense of faith,
16.26: Unto him Who alone is wise belongs The glory, by Yeshua The Messiah, for the eternity of eternities. Amen.
16.27: The grace of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah be with all of you. Amen.
